steps.title: Find the true subject
steps.content: The subject of the verb is Mathematician Grigori Perelman — a single person. The phrase "sometimes in conjunction with mathematicians Richard S. Hamilton and Shing-Tung Yau" is a parenthetical modifier (set off by commas) and does not change the subject's number.

steps.title: Match a singular verb
steps.content:
A singular subject takes a singular verb, so is credited is correct. "Have been," "are," and "are being" are all plural and would disagree with the singular subject Perelman.

That matches choice A — A is correct.
